macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Inflation Eases as Producer Prices Fall 0.3% in June, Fueling Fed Rate Cut Hopes
items.summary: U.S. wholesale prices unexpectedly declined 0.3% in June driven by a drop in gasoline, while the CPI fell 0.4% month-over-month, bringing the 12-month inflation rate to 3.5% and prompting Wall Street to close higher with major tech stocks like Amazon and Alphabet rising[1][7][8].

items.title: IBM Shares Plunge 25% in Single-Day 'Decapitation' After Earnings Miss
items.summary: IBM stock fell over $73 to approximately $217, a 25% single-day drop, following a significant earnings miss that triggered a massive sell-off in the tech sector[8].

items.title: PayPal Surges on Reports of $53 Billion Takeover Offer
items.summary: PayPal shares jumped significantly after reports emerged of a $53 billion takeover offer, adding to a deluge of corporate earnings activity that investors parsed alongside inflation data[1].

items.title: Major U.S. Indices Close Higher; Nasdaq Slumps Later Amid Tech Volatility
items.summary: Wall Street ended higher on cool inflation data and solid bank earnings, with the S&P 500 gaining 0.38%, though the tech-heavy Nasdaq later showed a notable slump as major averages moved lower in subsequent trading[2][3][7].

items.title: Bank of Korea Hikes Rates to 2.75% in First Increase Since 2022
items.summary: The Bank of Korea raised its benchmark rate by 25 basis points to 2.75%, marking the first rate hike in over three years to address inflation concerns, aligning with economist surveys[8].

items.title: Brent Crude Nears $85 Amid Renewed U.S.-Tehran Hostilities
items.summary: Oil prices climbed as Brent crude approached $85 a barrel due to renewed hostilities between Washington and Tehran, though WTI futures showed mixed movement earlier in the session[4][6].

items.title: Fed Beige Book: Soccer Tournament Not Translating to Broad Economic Growth
items.summary: The latest Federal Reserve Beige Book indicated that the recent soccer tournament did not necessarily translate into broader economic growth, despite a broadly improving economy with rising employment and easing inflation[1][8].

items.title: Brazil Raises 2026 Inflation Forecast to 5.1% as Tariff Threat Looms
items.summary: Brazil's government increased its inflation forecast for the year to 5.1% from 4.5%, while the country braces for potential new 25% U.S. tariffs on thousands of imports following unproductive negotiations[1].

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Above $65K as Soft US Inflation Data Eases Fed Rate-Hike Fears
items.summary: Bitcoin climbed 4.3% to trade near $65,025 after cooler-than-expected US inflation data reduced expectations for imminent Federal Reserve rate hikes, with prices reaching an intraday high of $65,507 before encountering resistance.

items.title: Ethereum Breaks $1,900 for First Time Since February Amid Market Rally
items.summary: Ethereum gained 5.3%, breaking the $1,900 threshold as the broader crypto market rose 3.2% to $2.22 trillion, driven by improving macro sentiment and institutional inflows.

items.title: Trump Meets Senators on CLARITY Act as Crypto Legislation Hopes Rise
items.summary: President Trump held a 'hugely positive' meeting with senators to discuss the CLARITY Act cryptocurrency legislation, raising expectations for its passage in the Senate, while Dick Durbin accused Todd Blanche of shielding Trump's crypto empire.

items.title: US Treasury and Tether Freeze $131M in Crypto Linked to Iran's Central Bank
items.summary: The US Treasury and Tether froze over $130 million in cryptocurrency tied to the Central Bank of Iran amid escalating Middle East tensions, marking a significant regulatory enforcement action.

items.title: BlackRock Scores Major SEC Win as IBIT Options Cap Quadruples
items.summary: BlackRock secured a significant SEC victory that quadruples the options cap for its IBIT Bitcoin ETF, while the firm also outlined a vision for crypto-TradFi convergence and joined DTCC's $114T tokenization push.

items.title: Jesse Pollak Hands Base App Leadership to Cobie After Admitting Social Bets Fell Short
items.summary: Coinbase's Jesse Pollak transferred Base app leadership to Cobie, acknowledging that social betting strategies underperformed, and is now pivoting Base's focus toward AI and trading applications.

items.title: CASHDOG Meme Coin Jumps 3,900% in Speculative Frenzy on Robinhood Chain
items.summary: CASHDOG experienced an extraordinary 3,900% price surge tied to the rapid growth of Robinhood Chain as a new meme coin hub, reflecting intense speculative activity in the sector.

items.title: Circle Wins $49M Arbitration Case Against Tether-Linked Heka Funds
items.summary: Circle won a major legal battle, securing a $49 million arbitration award against Heka Funds over USDC minting and redemption account disputes, reinforcing its legal standing against competitors.

items.title: Aave Launches V4 on Avalanche, Expanding RWA Lending Beyond Ethereum
items.summary: Aave deployed its V4 protocol on Avalanche, laying the groundwork for tokenized credit markets and expanding real-world asset (RWA) lending beyond the Ethereum ecosystem.

items.title: Stanford Study Exposes Polymarket Flaw Enabling Bitcoin Manipulation
items.summary: A Stanford study revealed a critical flaw in Polymarket's 5-minute Bitcoin prediction markets that allows for settlement manipulation, raising concerns about market integrity in decentralized prediction platforms.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab Debuts Open-Weight Inkling Model
items.summary: AI startup Thinking Machines Lab, led by former OpenAI CEO Mira Murati, released Inkling, an open-weight Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model with 975B total and 41B active parameters, designed to be broad rather than optimized for a single area [2][7].

items.title: Apple Intelligence Registered for Use in China with Local AI Integration
items.summary: China's cyberspace regulator registered Apple's on-device generative AI service 'Apple Intelligence' for iPhones in the country, where it will integrate Alibaba's Qwen and may add Baidu's AI features [1][7].

items.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Sonnet 5 and Restores Frontier Model Access
items.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Sonnet 5 and restored access to its Fable and Mythos frontier models after the Trump administration lifted export control restrictions tied to cybersecurity concerns [6][8].

items.title: OpenAI Launches Codex Micro Desktop Keypad for AI Agent Control
items.summary: OpenAI opened orders for Codex Micro, a limited-edition $230 desktop keypad built with Work Louder featuring backlit keys, a rotary knob, and a joystick to monitor and control AI agents [7].

items.title: Meta Announces $9.1B AI Data Center Investment in Canada
items.summary: Meta confirmed it will invest more than $13 billion Canadian ($9.1 billion) to build its first AI data center in Canada, marking its largest data center project in the country [8].

items.title: New York Becomes First US State to Halt Large Data Center Construction
items.summary: New York imposed a one-year moratorium on large new data center construction, becoming the first US state to do so amid concerns over rising power costs, water strain, and community burdens [1].

items.title: Google Makes Veo 3 Video Creator Available on Vertex AI
items.summary: Google released its most powerful AI video creator, Veo 3, for public use on its Vertex AI platform, enabling broader access to advanced video generation capabilities [6].

items.title: Ode with Anthropic Launches as $1.5B AI Implementation Firm
items.summary: The AI implementation company backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, and Hellman & Friedman launched under the name 'Ode with Anthropic' with 100 engineers to drive enterprise AI adoption [7].

items.title: Emergent Raises $130M Series C at $1.5B Valuation
items.summary: Indian AI coding startup Emergent secured $130 million in Series C funding at a $1.5 billion post-money valuation, a five-fold increase from its January valuation of $300 million [7].

items.title: Meta Introduces Muse Image Generation Model
items.summary: Meta introduced Muse Image, a new AI model allowing users to create and edit images, expanding its generative AI toolkit for creative applications [9].

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Raw response

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: TSMC Posts Record 77% Q2 Profit Jump, Beats Forecasts on AI Chip Demand
items.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC), the world's largest contract chipmaker, reported a 77% surge in second-quarter net profit, hitting a record as global demand for AI processors continues to boom.

items.title: TSMC Pledges Additional $100 Billion to Expand U.S. Chipmaking Capacity
items.summary: The Taiwanese chipmaker announced a new $100 billion investment to further expand its semiconductor manufacturing operations in the United States.

items.title: Amazon Launches Satellite Internet Service in South Africa
items.summary: Amazon is rolling out its satellite internet service in South Africa, reportedly beating Elon Musk's Starlink in Musk's home country.

items.title: Xiaomi Begins Rolling Out Stable Android 17-Based HyperOS 3 to Xiaomi 17 Series
items.summary: Xiaomi has started distributing stable updates of its HyperOS 3, based on Android 17, to the Xiaomi 17 series, including the June 2026 security patch.

items.title: New York Blocks New Large AI Data Centers for Up to One Year
items.summary: New York state will prohibit the construction of new large data centers that support artificial intelligence for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid.

items.title: India Cabinet Approves India Semiconductor Mission 2.0 to Boost Domestic Chip Manufacturing
items.summary: The Union Cabinet has cleared the India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) 2.0, a new initiative aimed at strengthening domestic semiconductor production capabilities.

items.title: Airbus Signs Multi-Year Cloud Infrastructure Deal with Scaleway for Defense Applications
items.summary: Airbus has entered a multi-year agreement with Iliad-owned Scaleway to provide cloud infrastructure for sensitive industrial and defense applications, supporting AI tool deployment.

items.title: Nokia and NVIDIA Unveil AI Platform to Double Network Capacity by 2028
items.summary: Nokia and NVIDIA have introduced a new AI-driven network technology partnership capable of doubling data transmission capacity by 2028.

items.title: NVIDIA Begins Shipping H200 AI Chips to China
items.summary: A U.S. official confirmed that NVIDIA's powerful H200 artificial intelligence chips have started shipping to China.

items.title: Meta Faces Lawsuit from 26 Employees Alleging AI-Driven Layoff Bias
items.summary: Twenty-six Meta employees have sued the company, claiming its AI systems unfairly targeted workers on medical and family leave for layoffs.
